title: Traefik EntryPoints Explained
description:
## Traefik EntryPoints Explained

Traefik uses **EntryPoints** as the first layer of its routing model.

Which of the following best describes what a Traefik EntryPoint is?

options.text: A Kubernetes Service that exposes Traefik to external traffic
A named network listener (port + protocol) where Traefik receives incoming connections
A middleware that rewrites incoming request URLs before routing
A Helm value that controls the number of Traefik replicas

explanation: An EntryPoint is a named network listener — it defines the port and protocol (TCP, UDP, HTTP) on which Traefik listens for incoming traffic. Common built-in names are `web` (port 80) and `websecure` (port 443). Routers then reference an EntryPoint to declare which listener should handle their traffic. EntryPoints are not Kubernetes resources; they are configured in Traefik's static configuration (Helm values or CLI flags).

hints.title: Checking available EntryPoints
hints.body: In a Helm-installed Traefik, EntryPoints are set via Helm values. You can inspect the running config via the Traefik API.
